message:
test(lifecycle-matrix): add row 5 — cleanup/quarantine inventory + dry-run reclamation proof

Extend the lifecycle validation matrix with a harness-level row that
freezes the evidence format the cleanup/quarantine worker must emit.

`cleanup_quarantine_inventory_trace_is_artifact_backed`:
- Four-beat LoadScript
  (idle:cleanup_inventory / idle:quarantine_detected /
   busy:foreground_pressure / idle:cleanup_resume) so each contention
  turn hits the expected machine-capacity shape.
- ContentionPlan with four turns:
    1. LexicalRepair/inventory — capture derivative asset inventory
       before the cleanup decision. Snapshot carries per-generation
       size / last_touched_at / reference_count records.
    2. LexicalRepair/quarantine — quarantine-decision evidence with
       a human-readable reason string (corruption fingerprint, FK
       mismatch, etc.) and a pointer to the flagged generation.
    3. ForegroundSearch/pause_cleanup — cleanup yields to foreground
       pressure. Snapshot records the pause_reason so the worker
       can be audited for responsiveness compliance.
    4. LexicalRepair/dry_run_resume — after pressure clears, the
       worker emits a dry-run reclamation verdict (bytes_reclaimable,
       generations_to_drop, safety_checks_passed) BEFORE any
       destructive action. The test asserts the snapshot carries
       the dry-run=true flag so the cleanup worker can't ship a
       version that collapses dry-run into real reclamation.

Harness-level until the real cleanup worker lands; matches the
row 4 (pause/resume trace) shape introduced in the previous
lifecycle-matrix commit. Both rows now document the invariants the
cleanup/quarantine subsystem must satisfy before shipping.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
